title: Cross-Service Dependency Map Discovery, Drift Detection, and Critical-Path Identification from Distributed Traces
dataSources: index=apm_traces sourcetype=otel:trace for OTLP-normalized spans with trace_id, span_id, parent_span_id, span.kind, service.name, peer.service, database and messaging semantic attributes; index=apm_metrics metric_name=trace.requests with sf_service and sf_dependent_service style dimensions for Splunk APM service map style aggregates; index=k8s sourcetype=k8s:metadata kind=Service for cluster service inventory; index=otel_metrics for otelcol_processor_servicegraph internal counters; index=synthetic_traces sourcetype=synthetic:trace for canary path validation; lookup service_dependency_baseline.csv keyed on caller and callee; index=cmdb sourcetype=service_inventory for CMDB enrichment
app: Splunk Observability Cloud APM, Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector, Grafana Tempo, Jaeger, AWS X-Ray mirrors, Kubernetes metadata inputs
description: Detects live service-to-service edge discovery from distributed traces, compares inferred adjacency to curated baselines and CMDB ownership, surfaces unapproved new edges especially toward data stores, flags fan-out blow-ups and sudden degree deltas, highlights bridge-like nodes using a centrality-style score, and contrasts disappearing critical-path edges against synthetic and Kubernetes structural anchors. The control merges multisearch lanes for raw parent-linked spans, APM dependency metrics, Kubernetes service inventory, collector servicegraph internals, and synthetic expectations, then applies drift, error, and recency logic for severity.
value: Architecture and platform leaders gain an accountable early warning when the real call graph diverges from what change boards approved, before shadow connectivity becomes an outage or compliance surprise. Product and SRE teams see which releases rewired critical paths, which services became brittle bridges, and which datastore dependencies appeared without catalog entries, enabling prioritized reviews instead of passive diagrams. Risk and finance partners receive evidence packs that tie unexpected edges to business units and SLA classes for governance forums.
grandmaExplanation: Picture a city map where new alleys appear overnight and familiar avenues vanish. We watch how software neighborhoods connect, flag surprise roads to sensitive places, noisy intersections with dozens of new exits, and missing bridges that used to carry rush-hour traffic, then we ask who approved the change before the whole city grid locks up.
implementation: Land otel:trace spans, APM dependency metrics, Kubernetes service metadata, collector servicegraph metrics, and optional synthetic traces into Splunk; maintain service_dependency_baseline.csv and cmdb service_inventory aligned to lowercase service keys. Save observability_uc_13_5_4_dependency_graph_drift on a five-minute cadence with earliest=-24h@h; route critical and high severities to platform observability on-call; archive weekly CSV exports for architecture governance.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control together with observability engineers, service architects, and production owners who maintain both the tracing pipelines and the authoritative service catalog. Before any graph drift alert is trusted, teams must agree how dependencies are represented in OpenTelemetry traces: span.kind separates client, server, internal, producer, and consumer roles; service.name identifies the logical service; peer.service names remote peers when present; db.system and related database semantic attributes name data stores; messaging.system names brokers; and HTTP or RPC semantic conventions describe networked calls. The OpenTelemetry semantic conventions for HTTP, RPC, and database spans define the attribute keys reviewers should expect in pull requests and collector transforms. W3C Trace Context still governs propagation across boundaries, and any loss at gateways, service meshes, or multi-cluster federation shows up as missing edges rather than magical latency improvements.

This use case is intentionally about live topology discovery and drift, not percentile latency alarms owned by UC-13.5.1, not error-rate-first paging owned by UC-13.5.2, not orphan or broken parent links owned by UC-13.5.3, not RED metric dashboards owned by UC-13.5.7, and not real-user Core Web Vitals owned by UC-13.5.9. It complements UC-13.3.7 by consuming traces already present without debating sampling yield mathematics; if sampling collapses, pair with UC-13.3.7 before interpreting edge disappearance as architecture truth. Legal and privacy reviews must cover peer.service values that can reveal customer tenants, database instance names, and internal project codenames. Role-based access control should limit trace indexes to engineering identities, segregate production from non-production with deployment.environment, and prevent casual export of full graph adjacency lists that fingerprint architecture.

Graph inference can proceed three ways in parallel: raw span parent_span_id linkage inside each trace, Splunk APM or vendor service map metrics that pre-aggregate caller and dependent pairs, and OpenTelemetry Collector servicegraph processor internal metrics that count edges they materialize for Prometheus or downstream dashboards. Grafana Tempo service graphs and Jaeger directed acyclic graph views provide interactive sanity checks when Splunk rows look surprising. Betweenness centrality and related graph metrics identify bridges that disproportionately connect communities; exact betweenness is expensive at full fleet scale, so the SPL uses a transparent bridge_score heuristic combining fan-out, fan-in, and log-weighted traffic while offline jobs in a graph notebook can compute true betweenness for monthly governance packs.

CMDB drift detection compares inferred edges to service_dependency_baseline.csv, a curated file with columns for caller, callee, edge_status, criticality, owner_team, sla_class, runbook_url, and business_unit. Edges marked expected_critical_path should never vanish silently; edges marked deprecated_should_remove should decay toward zero observed_call_rate_5min; edges missing from the baseline but touching data planes demand rapid review. High-cardinality externals such as fully qualified S3 endpoints should be collapsed with eval rules that strip bucket-specific DNS suffixes into stable service keys before stats, otherwise the graph becomes an unreadable star. Strangler-fig migrations often duplicate edges temporarily; require release correlation before treating duplicates as incidents. Service meshes like Istio and Linkerd inject additional client spans around the same logical hop; normalize mesh workload names to the same service.name labels used in application instrumentation or graphs will show parallel highways for one road. Cross-cluster Kubernetes federation and multi-region failover frequently expose gateway peering hops that architecture diagrams omit until networking teams update their sources of truth.

Risk briefing for leadership: a growing edge count is not automatically bad, but fan-out blow-up often signals feature work, batch fan-out, or runaway retry loops; disappearing edges on critical paths often mean broken propagation or real outages. Critical-path drift between releases should be reviewed in change advisory boards with Splunk APM service map screenshots, Tempo service graph exports, and this saved search CSV attached to the ticket.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A lands sourcetype=otel:trace spans in index=apm_traces with trace_id, span_id, parent_span_id, span.kind, service.name, duration fields normalized to milliseconds at search time if needed, database and messaging semantic attributes, and deployment.environment for partitioning. Preserve peer.service and net.peer.name when collectors emit them, but apply cardinality guards for cloud vendor DNS noise.

Lane B lands Splunk APM or mirrored trace.request style metrics in index=apm_metrics with dimensions usable as sf_service and sf_dependent_service when your metric schema follows Splunk Observability Cloud conventions; adjust field names in the mstats arm if your deployment uses service.name and dependent_service instead.

Lane C lands Kubernetes service inventory snapshots in index=k8s with sourcetype=k8s:metadata rows where kind=Service, carrying metadata.name, namespace, and cluster identifiers so platform teams can contrast live discovery against apiserver truth.

Lane D scrapes OpenTelemetry Collector internal metrics into index=otel_metrics, focusing on otelcol_processor_servicegraph counters that indicate how many edges the connector maintains and how many drops it sees when queues pressure.

Lane E lands synthetic canary traces in index=synthetic_traces with sourcetype=synthetic:trace listing expected_caller and expected_callee pairs so SRE teams can prove end-to-end propagation independent of customer traffic.

Maintain service_dependency_baseline.csv in a secured lookup directory with weekly refreshes from architecture repositories. Maintain cmdb sourcetype=service_inventory with service identifiers aligned to lowercase service graph keys. Document macros for index names so multi-tenant searches stay portable.

HTTP Event Collector tokens for trace JSON require rate monitoring; collector batching changes can skew five-minute buckets. Kubernetes metadata inputs should tolerate watch reconnects without duplicating services across buckets. For AWS X-Ray mirrored spans, map segment IDs into trace_id and parent relationships consistent with OpenTelemetry conventions so optional X-Ray-only arms can be added later without rewriting this search.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_5_4_dependency_graph_drift with a five-minute schedule in steady production, dispatch earliest=-24h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after volume guards on observed_call_rate_5min. Throttle duplicate caller, callee, and env tuples for twenty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, owner_team, and business_unit in pager narratives so incident commanders open the correct runbook without guessing.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans five lanes for raw parent-linked edges, APM service map metrics, Kubernetes inventory, collector servicegraph internals, and synthetic validators. coalesce ladders normalize missing caller, callee, env, and sla_class fields across bridges. stats aggregates observed_call_rate_5min, p99_latency, and error_rate proxies per bucket. eventstats computes fan_out_count and fan_in_count as degree proxies per time slice. streamstats supplies short hysteresis on fan_out_delta and last_seen timing for disappearance logic. inputlookup service_dependency_baseline.csv joins expected edge posture. join to cmdb service_inventory enriches ownership when baseline rows omit teams. case encodes critical severity when unreviewed datastore edges spike traffic, when fan-out exceeds fifty or delta jumps beyond ten, or when critical path edges go quiet beyond tolerance, with high and medium steps for error-heavy or migration-sensitive contexts.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.4 dependency graph drift fan-out centrality multisearch earliest=-24h@h bucket=5m")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=apm_traces sourcetype=otel:trace earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval span_kind=lower(toString(coalesce('span.kind',span_kind,"")))
      | where span_kind="client" OR like(span_kind,"%client%")
      | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,"unknown_caller"))))
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,traceId,"")
      | eval parent_span_id=coalesce(parent_span_id,parentSpanId,"")
      | eval dur_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,round('duration.nanos'/1000000,4),0))
      | eval is_err=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce('otel.status_code',otel_status_code,status.code,""))),"error") OR tonumber(coalesce(status.code,0),10)>=2,1,0)
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_env"))))
      | join type=left max=0 trace_id parent_span_id
          [ search index=apm_traces sourcetype=otel:trace earliest=-24h@h latest=now
            | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,traceId,"")
            | eval span_id=coalesce(span_id,spanId,"")
            | rename span_id AS parent_span_id
            | eval callee=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,"unknown_callee"))))
            | fields trace_id parent_span_id callee ]
      | where len(callee)>0 AND caller!=callee
      | eval lane="otel_parent_child_edge"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats count AS edge_events perc99(dur_ms) AS p99_latency sum(is_err) AS err_spans BY _time caller callee env lane ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS mv WHERE index=apm_metrics metric_name="trace.requests" earliest=-24h@h latest=now span=5m BY sf_service sf_dependent_service
      | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sf_service,"apm_caller"))))
      | eval callee=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sf_dependent_service,"apm_callee"))))
      | eval env="apm_metrics_plane"
      | eval edge_events=round(coalesce(mv,0),4)
      | eval p99_latency=0
      | eval err_spans=0
      | eval lane="apm_service_map_metric"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats sum(edge_events) AS edge_events max(p99_latency) AS p99_latency sum(err_spans) AS err_spans BY _time caller callee env lane ]
    [ search index=k8s sourcetype=k8s:metadata earliest=-24h@h latest=now kind=Service
      | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metadata_name,service_name,"k8s_service"))))
      | eval callee=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_namespace,namespace,"k8s_namespace"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_name,"k8s_cluster"))))
      | eval edge_events=1
      | eval p99_latency=0
      | eval err_spans=0
      | eval lane="k8s_service_inventory_snapshot"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats sum(edge_events) AS edge_events BY _time caller callee env lane ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS mv WHERE index=otel_metrics (metric_name="otelcol_processor_servicegraph_total_edges" OR metric_name="otelcol_processor_servicegraph_total_edges_dropped") earliest=-24h@h latest=now span=5m BY service_instance_id
      | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_instance_id,"otelcol"))))
      | eval callee="servicegraph_connector_aggregate"
      | eval env="collector_internal"
      | eval edge_events=round(coalesce(mv,0),4)
      | eval p99_latency=0
      | eval err_spans=0
      | eval lane="otelcol_servicegraph_internal"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats sum(edge_events) AS edge_events BY _time caller callee env lane ]
    [ search index=synthetic_traces sourcetype=synthetic:trace earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(expected_caller,synth_caller,"syn_caller"))))
      | eval callee=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(expected_callee,synth_callee,"syn_callee"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(synth_env,"synthetic"))))
      | eval edge_events=1
      | eval p99_latency=0
      | eval err_spans=0
      | eval lane="synthetic_path_validator"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats count AS edge_events BY _time caller callee env lane ]
| eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(caller,"unspecified_caller"))))
| eval callee=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(callee,"unspecified_callee"))))
| eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,"unknown_env"))))
| stats sum(edge_events) AS observed_call_rate_5min max(p99_latency) AS p99_latency sum(err_spans) AS err_total values(lane) AS lane_mv BY _time caller callee env
| eval error_rate=if(observed_call_rate_5min>0, round(err_total/observed_call_rate_5min,4), 0)
| sort 0 caller callee env _time
| eventstats dc(callee) AS fan_out_count BY caller env _time
| eventstats dc(caller) AS fan_in_count BY callee env _time
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f last(fan_out_count) AS prior_fan_out BY caller env
| eval fan_out_delta=coalesce(fan_out_count,0)-coalesce(prior_fan_out,0)
| streamstats window=24 current=f global=f earliest(_time) AS first_edge_time BY caller callee env
| eval edge_age_days=round((now()-first_edge_time)/86400,2)
| streamstats window=3 current=t global=f latest(_time) AS last_seen_ts BY caller callee env
| eval days_since_last_seen=round((now()-last_seen_ts)/86400,2)
| eventstats dc(caller) AS env_active_callers dc(callee) AS env_active_callees BY env _time
| eventstats sum(observed_call_rate_5min) AS env_edge_calls BY env _time
| join type=left max=0 caller callee
    [| inputlookup service_dependency_baseline.csv
     | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(caller)))
     | eval callee=lower(trim(toString(callee)))
     | fields caller callee edge_status criticality owner_team sla_class runbook_url business_unit ]
| join type=left max=0 caller
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=service_inventory earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_id,""))))
      | dedup caller
      | fields caller cmdb_tier cmdb_owner business_unit AS cmdb_business_unit ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,cmdb_owner,"unowned_team")
| eval business_unit=coalesce(business_unit,cmdb_business_unit,"unspecified_business_unit")
| eval sla_class=coalesce(sla_class,"standard")
| eval edge_status=coalesce(edge_status,"unreviewed_graph_edge")
| eval bridge_score=round((fan_out_count+fan_in_count)*log(observed_call_rate_5min+1),3)
| eval data_plane_touch=if(match(lower(callee),"(mysql|postgres|mongo|redis|cassandra|kafka|elasticsearch)") OR match(lower(caller),"(mysql|postgres|mongo|redis|cassandra|kafka|elasticsearch)"),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    data_plane_touch=1 AND match(edge_status,"unreviewed_graph_edge|missing_from_baseline") AND observed_call_rate_5min>=25, "critical",
    fan_out_count>=50 OR fan_out_delta>10, "critical",
    match(edge_status,"expected_critical_path") AND days_since_last_seen>1 AND observed_call_rate_5min<1, "critical",
    error_rate>=0.08 AND observed_call_rate_5min>=30, "high",
    match(edge_status,"deprecated_should_remove") AND observed_call_rate_5min>=10, "high",
    fan_out_delta>=6 AND observed_call_rate_5min>=100, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND data_plane_touch=1, "freeze_deploy_validate_cmdb_edge_owner_datastore_peer_and_otel_db_system_semconv",
    severity=="critical" AND fan_out_count>=50, "triage_fanout_blast_review_worker_pool_init_and_downstream_circuit_breakers",
    severity=="critical" AND days_since_last_seen>1, "restore_trace_propagation_mesh_sidecar_headers_and_parent_span_links",
    severity=="high", "open_service_map_compare_tempo_service_graph_jaeger_dag_validate_baseline_csv",
    true(), "record_graph_drift_update_architecture_diagram_and_synthetic_checks")
| table _time, caller, callee, owner_team, business_unit, observed_call_rate_5min, fan_out_count, edge_status, days_since_last_seen, p99_latency, error_rate, severity, recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_5_4_dependency_graph_drift_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_5_4_dependency_graph_drift | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance guidance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time rising, materialize each lane into five-minute summary indexes keyed on caller, callee, and env, widen operational alerts to read summaries, and retain this search for investigations. For very large meshes, split alerts by env and business_unit from lookup enrichment.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Splunk Observability Cloud APM service map or Grafana Tempo service graph views for a cluster you understand well. Pick a recent deployment window and confirm new edges in Splunk match visually discovered links within one collection interval. When Splunk disagrees with the UI, verify HEC latency, double-indexed bridges, and field aliases on deployment.environment.

Validate parent_span_id joins by sampling ten traces in Search, listing client and server spans, and manually confirming callee resolves. If callee is null, fix propagation or rename rules before trusting drift alerts.

Validate mstats arms against the metrics explorer for trace.requests or service map counters. Validate Kubernetes lane row counts against kubectl get service output for the same cluster. Validate collector servicegraph metrics by curling the collector self-telemetry endpoint and comparing counters to otel_metrics rows.

Validate lookup governance by running inputlookup service_dependency_baseline.csv alone and confirming tier-one services appear with edge_status and owner_team. Validate CMDB join coverage by measuring fill rates for cmdb_owner.

Synthetic drill: emit a synthetic trace with a new expected edge in non-production, confirm critical or high severity within two windows when baseline marks the edge as unreviewed, then add the edge to the baseline and confirm decay to low. Negative test: on a calm window with stable topology, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for thirty consecutive runs when thresholds are tuned.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — A payments API quietly opens a new database client span toward a previously unseen cluster after a migration cutover: compare db.system and peer.service attributes, reconcile CMDB data-store tiers, and block promotion until the baseline CSV lists the edge with owner and rollback steps.

Case 2 — A feature flag flips at noon and adds a fresh downstream gRPC call to a recommendations service: expect a new edge with medium severity until the architecture board records the dependency; pair with release metadata and flag audit logs.

Case 3 — A deprecated loyalty edge should vanish after decommission week but still shows steady traffic: treat as high severity when baseline marks deprecated_should_remove; chase strangler-fig routers still proxying old paths.

Case 4 — An Istio upgrade injects duplicate client spans so the inferred graph doubles HTTP edges: normalize on service.name, collapse mesh internal names, and compare to prior-week fan_out_count before paging.

Case 5 — A Linkerd sidecar misconfiguration drops outbound trace headers and parent links, making critical edges disappear for an hour: severity critical on expected_critical_path with flatlined calls; roll the proxy config and replay synthetic traces.

Case 6 — Chaos engineering injects a temporary fault between inventory and fulfillment, creating an ephemeral edge through a fallback catalog: annotate game-day windows and suppress paging when the chaos calendar matches the lane label.

Case 7 — A vendor SDK upgrade adds an exporter sidecar span that looks like a new external peer: validate peer.service normalization rules and strip high-cardinality AWS host suffixes into stable service keys.

Case 8 — A team forgets to delete an old payment-provider HTTP client during provider migration: two parallel edges appear; baseline drift shows duplicate money movement paths and should trigger finance governance review.

Case 9 — Aggressive bot traffic hits a public edge function and creates a spurious burst of calls to a marketing content API: apply bot scoring at the gateway and require sustained dwell before alerting on brand-new edges.

Case 10 — A nightly ETL coordinator fans out to fifty shard workers behind a single init operation, exploding fan_out_count at midnight: tag batch lanes, bucket schedules, and require delta from prior same-hour window before critical severity.

Case 11 — Cross-region failover peers two clusters and reveals a peering edge between regional gateways that the CMDB never listed: treat as configuration drift, capture networking change tickets, and update the baseline CSV with the peering hop.

Case 12 — A cron-only pricing recomputation service appears only at 00:15 local time: without schedule context the edge looks like a ghost dependency; enrich with Kubernetes CronJob metadata and seasonal markers in the lookup table.


Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, multisearch, inputlookup, and join appear; final table lists thirteen operational columns including severity and recommended_action for routing.

Teaching notes for junior engineers: always pair graph signals with change calendars and sampling health. A new edge can be good shipping, a security surprise, or a telemetry artifact; the baseline CSV and CMDB joins turn mystery into accountable ownership.

Finance and risk partners: export weekly CSVs of critical rows with business_unit and sla_class to show which unapproved datastore edges create audit exposure.

Runbook cross-links belong in service_dependency_baseline.csv runbook_url fields linking to internal architecture standards, mesh troubleshooting guides, and vendor status pages.

Post-incident reviews should ask whether fan-out spikes came from retries, batch shards, or real feature launches, and whether edge disappearance followed mesh upgrades, header stripping, or authentic service removal.

For multi-cloud estates, document which regions share collectors; imbalanced shards can bias edge counts per region even when global traffic looks steady.

Human judgment still validates automated severity; this search guides investigations, not replaces context during customer-impacting outages.

Additional depth on semantic normalization: HTTP spans should carry http.method, http.route or http.target, and server name attributes where available; RPC spans should carry rpc.system and rpc.service; database spans should carry db.system, db.name, and db.operation when statements are sanitized. When spans lack peer.service but include net.peer.name, derive a stable peer key with eval rules that strip trailing port numbers and replace regional DNS prefixes with logical service aliases maintained in a small lookup. Messaging spans should include messaging.system and destination names so broker edges do not collapse into anonymous TCP.

Additional depth on critical-path drift: compare weekly snapshots of the top ten edges by observed_call_rate_5min for tier-one flows such as checkout and account recovery. Store snapshots in a summary index or version-controlled CSV artifacts. Release tags from CI systems should annotate synthetic lane traffic so you can correlate graph deltas with build numbers without relying solely on wall clock time.

Additional depth on servicegraph processor behavior: the OpenTelemetry Collector servicegraph processor builds edges from spans that include client and server pairs; when it drops spans under load, internal metrics often rise on dropped edge counters while Splunk still sees partial raw spans—treat divergence between Lane A and Lane D as a pipeline health signal.

Additional depth on AWS X-Ray and Jaeger: when dual publishing traces, ensure segment and span ID encodings align with OpenTelemetry trace_id fields so optional future arms can join X-Ray service graphs to Splunk rows without rewriting dashboards. Jaeger DAG views help responders see instantaneous topology; use them to confirm bridge_score outliers before restructuring microservices.

Additional depth on Kubernetes service graphs: Kubernetes service objects describe intent, not observed calls; Lane C is a structural anchor, not proof of traffic. Combine with network policy audit logs when security teams question unexpected adjacency.

Additional depth on governance: require that every critical edge in the baseline CSV lists two human owners and a failover tested quarterly. Graph drift alerts should open tickets automatically when critical severity fires, using recommended_action codes as routing hints for automation platforms.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.5.4 dependency graph drift fan-out centrality multisearch earliest=-24h@h bucket=5m")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=apm_traces sourcetype=otel:trace earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval span_kind=lower(toString(coalesce('span.kind',span_kind,"")))
      | where span_kind="client" OR like(span_kind,"%client%")
      | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,"unknown_caller"))))
      | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,traceId,"")
      | eval parent_span_id=coalesce(parent_span_id,parentSpanId,"")
      | eval dur_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,round('duration.nanos'/1000000,4),0))
      | eval is_err=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce('otel.status_code',otel_status_code,status.code,""))),"error") OR tonumber(coalesce(status.code,0),10)>=2,1,0)
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_env"))))
      | join type=left max=0 trace_id parent_span_id
          [ search index=apm_traces sourcetype=otel:trace earliest=-24h@h latest=now
            | eval trace_id=coalesce(trace_id,traceId,"")
            | eval span_id=coalesce(span_id,spanId,"")
            | rename span_id AS parent_span_id
            | eval callee=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,"unknown_callee"))))
            | fields trace_id parent_span_id callee ]
      | where len(callee)>0 AND caller!=callee
      | eval lane="otel_parent_child_edge"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats count AS edge_events perc99(dur_ms) AS p99_latency sum(is_err) AS err_spans BY _time caller callee env lane ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS mv WHERE index=apm_metrics metric_name="trace.requests" earliest=-24h@h latest=now span=5m BY sf_service sf_dependent_service
      | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sf_service,"apm_caller"))))
      | eval callee=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sf_dependent_service,"apm_callee"))))
      | eval env="apm_metrics_plane"
      | eval edge_events=round(coalesce(mv,0),4)
      | eval p99_latency=0
      | eval err_spans=0
      | eval lane="apm_service_map_metric"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats sum(edge_events) AS edge_events max(p99_latency) AS p99_latency sum(err_spans) AS err_spans BY _time caller callee env lane ]
    [ search index=k8s sourcetype=k8s:metadata earliest=-24h@h latest=now kind=Service
      | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metadata_name,service_name,"k8s_service"))))
      | eval callee=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_namespace,namespace,"k8s_namespace"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_name,"k8s_cluster"))))
      | eval edge_events=1
      | eval p99_latency=0
      | eval err_spans=0
      | eval lane="k8s_service_inventory_snapshot"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats sum(edge_events) AS edge_events BY _time caller callee env lane ]
    [ | mstats sum(_value) AS mv WHERE index=otel_metrics (metric_name="otelcol_processor_servicegraph_total_edges" OR metric_name="otelcol_processor_servicegraph_total_edges_dropped") earliest=-24h@h latest=now span=5m BY service_instance_id
      | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_instance_id,"otelcol"))))
      | eval callee="servicegraph_connector_aggregate"
      | eval env="collector_internal"
      | eval edge_events=round(coalesce(mv,0),4)
      | eval p99_latency=0
      | eval err_spans=0
      | eval lane="otelcol_servicegraph_internal"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats sum(edge_events) AS edge_events BY _time caller callee env lane ]
    [ search index=synthetic_traces sourcetype=synthetic:trace earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(expected_caller,synth_caller,"syn_caller"))))
      | eval callee=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(expected_callee,synth_callee,"syn_callee"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(synth_env,"synthetic"))))
      | eval edge_events=1
      | eval p99_latency=0
      | eval err_spans=0
      | eval lane="synthetic_path_validator"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats count AS edge_events BY _time caller callee env lane ]
| eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(caller,"unspecified_caller"))))
| eval callee=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(callee,"unspecified_callee"))))
| eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,"unknown_env"))))
| stats sum(edge_events) AS observed_call_rate_5min max(p99_latency) AS p99_latency sum(err_spans) AS err_total values(lane) AS lane_mv BY _time caller callee env
| eval error_rate=if(observed_call_rate_5min>0, round(err_total/observed_call_rate_5min,4), 0)
| sort 0 caller callee env _time
| eventstats dc(callee) AS fan_out_count BY caller env _time
| eventstats dc(caller) AS fan_in_count BY callee env _time
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f last(fan_out_count) AS prior_fan_out BY caller env
| eval fan_out_delta=coalesce(fan_out_count,0)-coalesce(prior_fan_out,0)
| streamstats window=24 current=f global=f earliest(_time) AS first_edge_time BY caller callee env
| eval edge_age_days=round((now()-first_edge_time)/86400,2)
| streamstats window=3 current=t global=f latest(_time) AS last_seen_ts BY caller callee env
| eval days_since_last_seen=round((now()-last_seen_ts)/86400,2)
| eventstats dc(caller) AS env_active_callers dc(callee) AS env_active_callees BY env _time
| eventstats sum(observed_call_rate_5min) AS env_edge_calls BY env _time
| join type=left max=0 caller callee
    [| inputlookup service_dependency_baseline.csv
     | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(caller)))
     | eval callee=lower(trim(toString(callee)))
     | fields caller callee edge_status criticality owner_team sla_class runbook_url business_unit ]
| join type=left max=0 caller
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=service_inventory earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval caller=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_id,""))))
      | dedup caller
      | fields caller cmdb_tier cmdb_owner business_unit AS cmdb_business_unit ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,cmdb_owner,"unowned_team")
| eval business_unit=coalesce(business_unit,cmdb_business_unit,"unspecified_business_unit")
| eval sla_class=coalesce(sla_class,"standard")
| eval edge_status=coalesce(edge_status,"unreviewed_graph_edge")
| eval bridge_score=round((fan_out_count+fan_in_count)*log(observed_call_rate_5min+1),3)
| eval data_plane_touch=if(match(lower(callee),"(mysql|postgres|mongo|redis|cassandra|kafka|elasticsearch)") OR match(lower(caller),"(mysql|postgres|mongo|redis|cassandra|kafka|elasticsearch)"),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    data_plane_touch=1 AND match(edge_status,"unreviewed_graph_edge|missing_from_baseline") AND observed_call_rate_5min>=25, "critical",
    fan_out_count>=50 OR fan_out_delta>10, "critical",
    match(edge_status,"expected_critical_path") AND days_since_last_seen>1 AND observed_call_rate_5min<1, "critical",
    error_rate>=0.08 AND observed_call_rate_5min>=30, "high",
    match(edge_status,"deprecated_should_remove") AND observed_call_rate_5min>=10, "high",
    fan_out_delta>=6 AND observed_call_rate_5min>=100, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND data_plane_touch=1, "freeze_deploy_validate_cmdb_edge_owner_datastore_peer_and_otel_db_system_semconv",
    severity=="critical" AND fan_out_count>=50, "triage_fanout_blast_review_worker_pool_init_and_downstream_circuit_breakers",
    severity=="critical" AND days_since_last_seen>1, "restore_trace_propagation_mesh_sidecar_headers_and_parent_span_links",
    severity=="high", "open_service_map_compare_tempo_service_graph_jaeger_dag_validate_baseline_csv",
    true(), "record_graph_drift_update_architecture_diagram_and_synthetic_checks")
| table _time, caller, callee, owner_team, business_unit, observed_call_rate_5min, fan_out_count, edge_status, days_since_last_seen, p99_latency, error_rate, severity, recommended_action
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu latest(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance host=* earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS service_host
| join type=left max=0 service_host [| inputlookup service_dependency_baseline.csv | eval service_host=lower(trim(toString(caller))) | fields service_host owner_team business_unit]
| where cpu>0 OR mem>0
| table service_host cpu mem owner_team business_unit
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Performance summaries for collector and gateway hosts that participate in trace export; use Inventory-aligned lookups for service rows keyed to service_dependency_baseline.csv and cmdb service_inventory; retain thirty days for quarterly topology attestations.
visualization: Force-directed dependency graph fed by exported edge lists, heatmap of fan_out_count by caller, timeline of observed_call_rate_5min for new datastore edges, table of severity-colored rows with drilldowns to Tempo service graph and Jaeger DAG screenshots, single-value tiles counting critical drift incidents per business_unit.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Intro to Splunk Observability Cloud APM
Grafana Tempo — Metrics generator service graphs
Kubernetes — Service concept (cluster service graph anchor)

knownFalsePositives: Legitimate canary deployments and blue-green cutovers often introduce temporary edges that resemble shadow connectivity until traffic fully shifts; require sustained dwell and release correlation before paging executives. Planned strangler-fig migrations intentionally duplicate old and new edges for weeks; baseline CSV annotations should mark those tuples as expected_duplicate with an expiry date. Scheduled nightly batch workloads create burst fan-out that looks like an incident if you ignore cron context; compare to the same clock hour on prior days. On-demand partner APIs that fire only during monthly closes can look like fresh edges when they wake up; enrich with contract calendars. Vendor IP rotation and DNS load balancing can change peer.service strings while the logical dependency is unchanged; collapse DNS noise with normalization rules before alerting. Ephemeral integration tests in shared staging clusters may fabricate rare edges; segregate environments in lookup tables. Service mesh debug endpoints occasionally emit diagnostic client spans that resemble new externals; filter known mesh housekeeping operations. Regional failover drills can swing edges across peering hops without any application code deploy; treat drills as planned when change calendars include them.
hardware: Kubernetes-hosted microservices instrumented with OpenTelemetry across Istio or Linkerd meshes, exporting traces through OpenTelemetry Collector to Splunk HTTP Event Collector or Observability Cloud, with optional Grafana Tempo and Jaeger mirrors for interactive graph validation.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.5.1 latency percentile anomaly detection, UC-13.5.2 error-rate-led paging, UC-13.5.3 orphan-span completeness analytics, UC-13.5.7 RED metric dashboards, UC-13.5.9 real-user Core Web Vitals, or UC-13.3.7 sampling yield and bias detection. Does not automatically mutate Kubernetes network policies or CMDB records.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab tenant, ingest otel:trace events where a tier-one service begins calling a new database peer with steady observed_call_rate_5min above thresholds while service_dependency_baseline.csv lacks the edge; confirm observability_uc_13_5_4_dependency_graph_drift returns critical within two scheduled runs with non-null recommended_action.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After baseline updates and mesh fixes when fan-out and error_rate return to calm patterns for ninety minutes with synthetic lane success, confirm severities return to low or medium and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_5_4_dependency_graph_drift with scheduled execution, exported results to a restricted observability_evidence index, and dashboard drilldowns tied to APM service maps and architecture repository tickets.
